title: DNS Resolution, Address Classes, Redirects, Proxies, and SSRF · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for dns resolution, address classes, redirects, proxies, and ssrf and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for validated connection-time dial policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Allowlist HTTP schemes and ports, reject userinfo and ambiguous host syntax, and prohibit unsupported protocols.
Use net/netip to classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Bind admitted addresses to DialContext or a controlled egress proxy and verify each new connection and re-resolution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
